name: Devilled Clams
recipeIngredient: 12 LARGE Quahog Clams Cored, Seeded & Minced
1/4 Cup Green Bell Pepper, Finely Diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees.
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
3. Steam the clams in the boiling water until they open (about 5 minutes).
4. Cool the steamed clams and reserve some of the liquid for later use.
5. Remove the clams from their shells and chop them finely, reserving the shells.
6. Stir in the bread crumbs, salt, and pepper to taste, then set aside.
7. Cook the bacon until crisp, drain it, and crumble into small pieces.
8. Sauté the garlic, green bell pepper, and onion (or minced onion) in the bacon fat until the vegetables are transparent.
9. Combine the sautéed vegetables with the clam mixture.
10. Stir in the reserved liquid from the steamed clams and just enough clam juice to moisten the mixture.
11. Add the chopped parsley and stuff the mixture into the clam shells.
12. Top each shell with crumbled bacon and additional parsley, then dot lightly with butter.
13. Bake until warmed through (3-5 minutes).
14. Serve hot.
